messages.content:
in column A all the cells are text.
Some of these text cells read as such; Monday 31st July 2023, Wednesday 2nd August 2023, Saturday 19th August 2023, Wedneday 23rd August 2023, etc.
How can I copy these text values to Column B as Date format.

To copy the text values in Column A as date format to Column B, you can use the following steps:

1. Select the range of cells in Column A that contain the text values you want to copy (e.g., A1:A5).

2. Right-click on the selected range and choose "Copy" or press "Ctrl+C" to copy the values.

3. Select the first cell in Column B where you want to paste the date values (e.g., B1).

4. Right-click on the selected cell and choose "Paste Special" or press "Ctrl+Shift+V".

5. In the "Paste Special" dialog box, select the "Values" option to paste only the values.

6. Click on the "OK" button to paste the values in Column B.

Note: The text values in Column B will still appear as text until you convert them to the date format. To convert the text values to dates, follow these additional steps:

7. Select the range of cells in Column B that contains the copied text values you want to convert to dates (e.g., B1:B5).

8. Right-click on the selected range and choose "Format Cells" or press "Ctrl+1".

9. In the "Format Cells" dialog box, select the "Date" category from the list on the left.

10. Choose the desired date format from the list on the right or specify a custom format if needed.

11. Click on the "OK" button to apply the date format to the selected cells in Column B.

Now, the text values in Column A will be copied and converted to the date format in Column B.
how can this be done using a formula

To convert the text values in Column A to dates in Column B using a formula, you can use the DATEVALUE function. Here are the steps:

1. In cell B1, enter the following formula:
   =DATEVALUE(A1)

2. Press Enter to get the converted date value for the first cell.

3. Now, click on cell B1 to select it, and then click on the small square handle in the bottom-right corner of the cell.

4. Drag the handle down to copy the formula to the rest of the cells in Column B corresponding to the range of text values in Column A.

The DATEVALUE function will convert the text values in Column A to their corresponding date values in Column B. Make sure to format the cells in Column B as date format to display the converted values correctly.
The formula did not work. I am trying to convert 'Monday 31st July' in column A3 to appear in B3 as a date

Apologies for the confusion. Since the text values you provided do not include the year, we need to add a year for the DATEVALUE function to work correctly. Let's assume we want to use the year 2023 for all the dates. Here's how you can modify the formula:

In cell B3, enter the following formula:
=DATEVALUE(LEFT(A3,LEN(A3)-9)&" 2023")

This formula extracts the date portion of the text value in cell A3 by using the LEFT and LEN functions. Then, it concatenates " 2023" to the extracted date to form a complete date string. The DATEVALUE function converts this date string to a date value.

Once you enter the formula in cell B3, you can drag it down to copy the formula for the rest of the cells in Column B. The text values in Column A will be converted to dates in Column B, assuming the format of the text values remains consistent throughout.
Using the formula I get a #VALUE! error

Apologies for the confusion. If you're getting a #VALUE! error, it's likely due to the date format in the text cells not being recognizable by the DATEVALUE function. 

To overcome this, we can utilize a combination of functions to extract the day, month, and year separately, and then use the DATE function to convert it into a date format.

In cell B3, enter the following formula:
```
=DATE(RIGHT(A3,4),MID(A3,FIND(" ",A3)+1,FIND(" ",A3,FIND(" ",A3)+1)-FIND(" ",A3)-1),LEFT(A3,FIND(" ",A3)-1))
```

This formula extracts the year, month, and day from the text value in cell A3 and constructs a date format using the DATE function. 

Once you enter the formula in cell B3, you can drag it down to copy the formula for the rest of the cells in Column B. The text values in Column A will now be converted to dates in Column B.

Please ensure the text values in column A consistently follow the format provided in your question for this formula to work accurately.
Using the formula I get a #VALUE! error

Apologies for the confusion again. Here's an updated formula that should work for your specific scenario:

In cell B3, enter the following formula:
=DATE(2023,MONTH(DATEVALUE(MID(A3,FIND(" ",A3)+1,LEN(A3)))) ,LEFT(A3,FIND(" ",A3)-3))

This formula assumes that the year is fixed as 2023 for all the dates and will extract the day, month, and year from the text values in Column A.

Once you enter the formula in cell B3, you can drag it down to copy the formula for the rest of the cells in Column B. The text values in Column A will be converted to dates in Column B.

Please ensure the text values in column A consistently follow the format you provided in your question for this formula to work accurately.

In column A all the cells are text.
Some of these text cells read as such; Monday 31st July 2023, Wednesday 2nd August 2023, Saturday 19th August 2023, Wedneday 23rd August 2023, etc.
How can I copy the First word to column B, if the word is either, Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday or Sunday

To copy the first word from each cell in Column A to Column B, if the word is Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, or Sunday, you can use the following formula:

In cell B1, enter the following formula:
=IF(OR(LEFT(A1,LEN("Monday"))="Monday",LEFT(A1,LEN("Tuesday"))="Tuesday",LEFT(A1,LEN("Wednesday"))="Wednesday",LEFT(A1,LEN("Thursday"))="Thursday",LEFT(A1,LEN("Friday"))="Friday",LEFT(A1,LEN("Saturday"))="Saturday",LEFT(A1,LEN("Sunday"))="Sunday"),LEFT(A1,FIND(" ",A1)-1),"")

This formula checks if the first word in each cell in Column A matches any of the specified days. If there is a match, it extracts the first word using the LEFT and FIND functions. If there is no match, it returns an empty string.

Once you enter the formula in cell B1, you can drag it down to copy the formula for the rest of the cells in Column B. The first words from the cells in Column A that match the specified days will be copied to Column B, with empty cells for non-matching words.

In column B, I have Values spaced apart by varying number of empty cells.
The values follow the sequence Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday and Sunday, repeating the sequence.
The values start from B3 and do not necessary start with the value Monday. B3 may start with the value Thursday.
Irrespective of the value in B3, the sequence continues and repeats as it goes down the column B. The values will never go past B900.
I want a VBA code that will go down the column from B3 to B900. Check the cells that are not empty and if the non-empty cell does not follow the sequence then the Cell must be highlighted Yellow.
After the disruption of the sequence and the highlight of the cell, the code will continue searching downwards using the highlighted cell value ast the start of the new sequence.

Sure! You can use the following VBA code to achieve this:

```vba
Sub HighlightSequenceDisruption()
    Dim ws As Worksheet
    Set ws = ThisWorkbook.Worksheets("Sheet1") ' Replace "Sheet1" with your actual sheet name
    
    Dim startCell As Range
    Set startCell = ws.Range("B3") ' Replace "B3" with the starting cell address in Column B
    
    Dim currentCell As Range
    Set currentCell = startCell
    Dim currentDay As Variant
    currentDay = currentCell.Value
    
    Dim nextDay As Variant
    
    'Loop through the range from startCell to B900
    Do Until currentCell.Row > 900
        'Find the next non-empty cell
        Set currentCell = currentCell.End(xlDown)
        If IsEmpty(currentCell) Then Exit Sub ' If reached an empty cell, exit the loop
        
        'Check if the value follows the sequence
        nextDay = WorksheetFunction.Index(Array("Monday", "Tuesday", "Wednesday", "Thursday", "Friday", "Saturday", "Sunday"), WorksheetFunction.Match(currentDay, Array("Monday", "Tuesday", "Wednesday", "Thursday", "Friday", "Saturday", "Sunday"), 0) + 1)
        
        If currentCell.Value <> nextDay Then
            ' Highlight the cell if it does not follow the sequence
            currentCell.Interior.Color = RGB(255, 255, 0) ' Change the RGB color code as per your preference
        End If
        
        currentDay = currentCell.Value
    Loop
End Sub
```

To use this code:

1. Press `ALT+F11` to open the VBA editor.
2. Insert a new module by clicking "Insert" > "Module".
3. Paste the code into the module.
4. Modify the code to reflect your worksheet name and starting cell address, if necessary.
5. Run the code by pressing `F5` or `Run` > `Run Sub/UserForm`.
6. The code will go down the specified range in Column B and highlight cells in yellow if they don't follow the sequence.

Please note that this code assumes the sequence starts from the specified starting cell and continues downwards. It uses the INDEX and MATCH functions to determine the next day in the sequence. You can adjust the sequence and starting cell as needed.

In column B most of the cells are empty. 
However, some cells have values and these cells are spaced apart by varying number of empty cells.
The values follow a repeating sequence Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday, Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday.
The first non empty call starts from B3 but it does not necessary start with the value Monday. B3 may start with the value Thursday and in this instance the next non empty cell should be Friday if it s to follow the sequence.
Irrespective of the value in B3, the sequence continues and repeats as it goes down the column B. The values will never go past B1000.
I want a VBA code that will go down the column from B3 to B1000.
Find the cells that are not empty and if the next non-empty cell does not follow the sequence then the Cell must be highlighted Yellow.
After finding the break of the sequence and highlighting of the cell, the code must continue searching downwards using the next non empty cell value as the start of the new sequence search.
Here is an example of a break in the sequence;
Monday, Tuesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday. Wednesday is missing.
The sequence should have been; Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday.
Another example of a break in the sequence is;
Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday, Monday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday. Tuesday is missing.

Certainly! Here's an updated VBA code that will go through Column B from B3 to B1000, find any breaks in the sequence, and highlight the cells in yellow where the sequence is disrupted:

```vba
Sub HighlightSequenceDisruption()
    Dim ws As Worksheet
    Set ws = ThisWorkbook.Worksheets("Sheet1") ' Replace "Sheet1" with your actual sheet name
    
    Dim startCell As Range
    Set startCell = ws.Range("B3") ' Replace "B3" with the starting cell address in Column B
    
    Dim currentCell As Range
    Set currentCell = startCell
    Dim currentDay As Variant
    currentDay = currentCell.Value
    
    Dim nextDay As Variant
    
    ' Loop through the range from startCell to B1000
    Do Until currentCell.Row > 1000
        ' Find the next non-empty cell
        Set currentCell = currentCell.End(xlDown)
        If IsEmpty(currentCell) Then Exit Sub ' If reached an empty cell, exit the loop
        
        ' Check if the value follows the sequence
        nextDay = WorksheetFunction.Index(Array("Monday", "Tuesday", "Wednesday", "Thursday", "Friday", "Saturday", "Sunday"), (WorksheetFunction.Match(currentDay, Array("Monday", "Tuesday", "Wednesday", "Thursday", "Friday", "Saturday", "Sunday"), 0) Mod 7) + 1)
        
        If currentCell.Value <> nextDay Then
            ' Highlight the cell if it does not follow the sequence
            currentCell.Interior.Color = RGB(255, 255, 0) ' Change the RGB color code as per your preference
        End If
        
        currentDay = currentCell.Value
    Loop
End Sub
```

To use this code:

1. Press `ALT+F11` to open the VBA editor.
2. Insert a new module by clicking "Insert" > "Module".
3. Paste the code into the module.
4. Modify the code to reflect your worksheet name and starting cell address, if necessary.
5. Run the code by pressing `F5` or `Run` > `Run Sub/UserForm`.
6. The code will go down the specified range in Column B and highlight cells in yellow if they don't follow the sequence.

This updated code uses a modulo operation to handle the repeating sequence properly. It calculates the next day based on the remainder of the current day's position in the sequence rather than using a fixed index.

Please note that this code assumes the sequence starts from the specified starting cell and continues downwards. It highlights cells that do not follow the sequence in yellow. Adjust the sequence and starting cell as needed.
I am getting a subscript out of range error

I apologize for the inconvenience. The error is occurring because the code assumes that the starting cell is non-empty. To resolve this issue, you can modify the code to check for the first non-empty cell in the specified range. 

Please use the updated code below:

```vba
Sub HighlightSequenceDisruption()
    Dim ws As Worksheet
    Set ws = ThisWorkbook.Worksheets("Sheet1") ' Replace "Sheet1" with your actual sheet name
    
    Dim startCell As Range
    Set startCell = ws.Range("B3") ' Replace "B3" with the starting cell address in Column B
    
    Dim currentCell As Range
    Set currentCell = ws.Range(startCell, startCell.End(xlDown)).Find("*", LookIn:=xlValues, SearchDirection:=xlNext)
    
    Dim currentDay As Variant
    currentDay = currentCell.Value
    
    Dim nextDay As Variant
    
    ' Loop through the range from the first non-empty cell to B1000
    Do Until currentCell.Row > 1000
        ' Find the next non-empty cell
        Set currentCell = currentCell.Offset(1).End(xlDown)
        If IsEmpty(currentCell) Then Exit Sub ' If reached an empty cell, exit the loop
        
        ' Check if the value follows the sequence
        nextDay = WorksheetFunction.Index(Array("Monday", "Tuesday", "Wednesday", "Thursday", "Friday", "Saturday", "Sunday"), (WorksheetFunction.Match(currentDay, Array("Monday", "Tuesday", "Wednesday", "Thursday", "Friday", "Saturday", "Sunday"), 0) Mod 7) + 1)
        
        If currentCell.Value <> nextDay Then
            ' Highlight the cell if it does not follow the sequence
            currentCell.Interior.Color = RGB(255, 255, 0) ' Change the RGB color code as per your preference
        End If
        
        currentDay = currentCell.Value
    Loop
End Sub
```

Please make sure to replace "Sheet1" with the name of your actual worksheet and adjust the starting cell address if needed. The code will now find the first non-empty cell in the specified range and use it as the starting point to search for the sequence.

Let me know if you have any further issues!
I am not getting any errors, but the code is not finding the cell value that does not match the sequence
